prompt: Japanese ukiyo-e woodblock print, Edo period (late 18th century), in the manner of Katsushika Hokusai’s landscape series. A solitary traveler descends a steep, winding mountain path that continuously flows downward across multiple layered ridges into a deep valley basin, each segment clearly lower than the previous. Strong diagonal composition from upper right to lower left guiding the eye downward. Flat, unmodulated color fields with no gradients or shading. Bold, clean black contour lines of consistent thickness. Limited palette of indigo blue, muted ochre, pale cream, and soft grey. Layered distant mountains rendered as flat overlapping shapes with no perspective shading. Visible woodblock grain texture. No modern elements, no text, no symbols, no photographic lighting.
tradition_approach: Applied Hokusai-style diagonal composition, flat unshaded color fields, bold uniform outlines, limited indigo-ochre palette, and layered flat mountain forms to reflect ukiyo-e spatial grammar.
